Name: Pinsir
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon clamps its pincers down on its prey and then either splits the prey in half or flings it away.

Name: Mega Pinsir ex
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 70 more damage.

Name: Wurmple
Flavor-Text: It lives among the tall grass and in forests. It repels attacks by raising up the spikes on its rear.

Name: Silcoon
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks if that damage is 40 or less.
Flavor-Text: It drinks dew that collects on its silk and waits for evolution. Its hard cocoon repels attacks.

Name: Beautifly
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has an aggressive nature. It stabs prey with its long, narrow mouth to drain the prey’s fluids.

Name: Cascoon
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks if that damage is 40 or less.
Flavor-Text: It never forgets any attack it endured while in the cocoon. After evolution, it seeks payback.

Name: Dustox
Effects: Choose either Poisoned or Confused. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now affected by that Special Condition.
Flavor-Text: It violently flutters its wings to scatter toxic dust when attacked. It becomes active after sunset.

Name: Seedot
Flavor-Text: It attaches itself to a tree branch using the top of its head. Strong winds can sometimes make it fall.

Name: Nuzleaf
Flavor-Text: They live in holes bored in large trees. The sound of Nuzleaf’s grass flute fills listeners with dread.

Name: Shiftry
Effects: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.
Flavor-Text: It lives quietly in the deep forest. It is said to create chilly winter winds with the fans it holds.

Name: Shroomish
Flavor-Text: If it senses danger, it scatters spores from the top of its head to protect itself.

Name: Breloom
Flavor-Text: The seeds on its tail are made of toxic spores. It knocks out foes with quick, virtually invisible punches.

Name: Pansage
Flavor-Text: It’s good at finding berries and gathers them from all over. It’s kind enough to share them with friends.

Name: Simisage
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Ill tempered, it fights by swinging its barbed tail around wildly. The leaf growing on its head is very bitter.

Name: Cottonee
Flavor-Text: It shoots cotton from its body to protect itself. If it gets caught up in hurricane-strength winds, it can get sent to the other side of the Earth.

Name: Whimsicott ex
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

Name: Petilil
Flavor-Text: If the leaves on its head are pruned with regularity, this Pokémon can be grown into a fine plump shape.

Name: Lilligant
Ability-Effect: Each of your [G] Pokémon gets +20 HP.
Flavor-Text: No matter how much time and money is spent raising it, its flowers are the most beautiful when they bloom in the wild.

Name: Maractus
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Arid regions are their habitat. They move rhythmically, making a sound similar to maracas.

Name: Virizion
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Sacred Sword.
Flavor-Text: It darts around opponents with a flurry of quick movements, slicing them up with its horns.

Name: Skiddo
Flavor-Text: As long as it has sunlight and water, Skiddo can make energy with the leaves on its body, allowing it to live on rocky mountains barren of food.

Name: Gogoat
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon live in herds in mountainous places. The victor in their contests of clashing horns will become the leader of the herd.

Name: Phantump
Flavor-Text: With a voice like a human child’s, it cries out to lure adults deep into the forest, getting them lost among the trees.

Name: Trevenant
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Small roots that extend from the tips of this Pokémon’s feet can tie into the trees of the forest and give Trevenant control over them.

Name: Grookey
Flavor-Text: It attacks with rapid beats of its stick. As it strikes with amazing speed, it gets more and more pumped.

Name: Thwackey
Flavor-Text: When it’s drumming out rapid beats in battle, it gets so caught up in the rhythm that it won’t even notice that it’s already knocked out its opponent.

Name: Rillaboom
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may flip a coin. If heads, switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.
Flavor-Text: The one with the best drumming techniques becomes the boss of the troop. It has a gentle disposition and values harmony among its group.

Name: Growlithe
Flavor-Text: It’s very friendly and faithful to people. It will try to repel enemies by barking and biting.

Name: Arcanine
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: Its magnificent bark conveys a sense of majesty. Anyone hearing it can’t help but grovel before it.

Name: Ponyta
Flavor-Text: If you’ve been accepted by Ponyta, its burning mane is mysteriously no longer hot to the touch.

Name: Rapidash ex
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

Name: Ho-Oh
Effects: Heal 30 damage from each of your Benched Basic Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It will reveal itself before a pure-hearted Trainer by shining its bright, rainbow-colored wings.

Name: Torchic
Flavor-Text: A fire burns inside, so it feels very warm to hug. It launches fireballs of 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Combusken
Flavor-Text: During a battle, the hot flame in its body increases. Its kicks have outstanding destructive power.

Name: Blaziken
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When facing a tough foe, it looses flames from its wrists. Its powerful legs let it jump clear over buildings.

Name: Mega Blaziken ex
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy from this Pokémon. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

Name: Pansear
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon lives in caves in volcanoes. The fire within the tuft on its head can reach 600 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Simisear
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: When it gets excited, embers rise from its head and tail and it gets hot. For some reason, it loves sweets.

Name: Darumaka
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This popular symbol of good fortune will never fall over in its sleep, no matter how it’s pushed or pulled.

Name: Darmanitan
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon’s power level rises along with the temperature of its fire, which can reach 2,500 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Litwick
Flavor-Text: The younger the life this Pokémon absorbs, the brighter and eerier the flame on its head burns.

Name: Lampent
Flavor-Text: It lurks in cities, pretending to be a lamp. Once it finds someone whose death is near, it will trail quietly after them.

Name: Chandelure
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may discard the top card of your opponent's deck.
Flavor-Text: In homes illuminated by Chandelure instead of lights, funerals were a constant occurrence— or so it’s said.

Name: Heatmor
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A flame serves as its tongue, melting through the hard shell of Durant so that Heatmor can devour their insides.

Name: Litleo
Flavor-Text: The more a Litleo trains its body and spirit by battling mighty enemies, the hotter its mane will grow.

Name: Pyroar
Effects: Discard 2 [R] Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The mane of a male Pyroar heats up to over 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit during battle. Merely approaching it will cause severe burns.

Name: Turtonator
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack, do 20 damage to the Attacking Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Eating sulfur in its volcanic habitat is what causes explosive compounds to develop in its shell. Its droppings are also dangerously explosive.

Name: Psyduck
Flavor-Text: If its chronic headache peaks, it may exhibit odd powers. It seems unable to recall such an episode.

Name: Golduck
Flavor-Text: It swims gracefully along on the quiet, slow- moving rivers and lakes of which it is so fond.

Name: Magikarp
Effects: Put a random card from your deck that evolves from this Pokémon onto this Pokémon to evolve it.
Flavor-Text: In the distant past, it was somewhat stronger than the horribly weak descendants that exist today.

Name: Gyarados
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.
Flavor-Text: It appears whenever there is world conflict, burning down any place it travels through.

Name: Mega Gyarados ex
Effects: Discard the top 3 cards of your opponent's deck.

Name: Lotad
Flavor-Text: Its leaf grew too large for it to live on land. That is how it began to live floating in the water.

Name: Lombre
Flavor-Text: It lives at the water’s edge where it is sunny. It sleeps on a bed of water grass by day and becomes active at night.

Name: Ludicolo
Effects: If you have exactly 1, 3, or 5 cards in your hand, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: If it hears festive music, it begins moving in rhythm in order to amplify its power.

Name: Wailmer
Flavor-Text: When it sucks in a large volume of seawater, it becomes like a big, bouncy ball. It eats a ton of food daily.

Name: Wailord
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The biggest of all Pokémon. It can dive to a depth of almost 10,000 feet on only one breath.

Name: Corphish
Flavor-Text: It was originally a Pokémon from afar that escaped to the wild. It can adapt to the dirtiest river.

Name: Crawdaunt
Flavor-Text: A brutish Pokémon that loves to battle. It will crash itself into any foe that approaches its nest.

Name: Luvdisc
Effects: If you have exactly 2, 4, or 6 cards in your hand, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: During the spawning season, countless Luvdisc congregate at coral reefs, turning the waters pink.

Name: Panpour
Flavor-Text: The water stored inside the tuft on its head is full of nutrients. Plants that receive its water grow large.

Name: Simipour
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It prefers places with clean water. When its tuft runs low, it replenishes it by siphoning up water with its tail.

Name: Tympole
Flavor-Text: It uses sound waves to communicate with others of its kind. People and other Pokémon species can’t hear its cries of warning.

Name: Palpitoad
Flavor-Text: On occasion, their cries are sublimely pleasing to the ear. Palpitoad with larger lumps on their bodies can sing with a wider range of sounds.

Name: Seismitoad
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is popular among the elderly, who say the vibrations of its lumps are great for massages.

Name: Tirtouga
Flavor-Text: Tirtouga is considered to be the ancestor of many turtle Pokémon. It was restored to life from a fossil.

Name: Carracosta
Effects: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon emerges from the water in search of prey despite the fact that it moves more slowly on land.

Name: Frillish
Flavor-Text: Legend has it that the residents of a sunken ancient city changed into these Pokémon.

Name: Jellicent
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, take a [W] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Whenever a full moon hangs in the night sky, schools of Jellicent gather near the surface of the sea, waiting for their prey to appear.

Name: Keldeo
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Sacred Sword.
Flavor-Text: It crosses the world, running over the surfaces of oceans and rivers. It appears at scenic waterfronts.

Name: Froakie
Flavor-Text: It protects its skin by covering its body in delicate bubbles. Beneath its happy-go-lucky air, it keeps a watchful eye on its surroundings.

Name: Frogadier
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its swiftness is unparalleled. It can scale a tower of more than 2,000 feet in a minute’s time.

Name: Greninja ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may switch your Active [W] Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

Name: Bergmite
Flavor-Text: They live in mountainous regions of frigid cold. On rare occasions, they ride on the back of an Avalugg to cross seas and move to new habitats.

Name: Avalugg
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon uses its massive icy body to flatten anything that gets in its way. When it’s floating out on the ocean, it looks exactly like drift ice.

Name: Chewtle
Flavor-Text: A popular game among children is to lift up sticks that Chewtle have bitten onto to see whose Chewtle can hang on the longest.

Name: Drednaw
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.
Flavor-Text: Drednaw lurks along the shoreline. When prey come to drink water, Drednaw stretches its neck out and chomps down on them.

Name: Arrokuda
Flavor-Text: It takes down prey by charging into them with its hard, pointed jaw. But Arrokuda’s eyesight is poor, so this tactic has a low success rate.

Name: Barraskewda
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It spins its tail fins to propel itself, surging forward at speeds of over 100 knots before ramming prey and spearing into them.

Name: Eiscue
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has full HP, it takes −40 damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: If you lick the ice covering its face, you’ll find it has a faintly salty taste. This species rode here on ocean currents from a cold, faraway land.

Name: Jolteon ex
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, whenever your opponent attaches an Energy from their Energy Zone to 1 of their Pokémon, do 20 damage to that Pokémon.

Name: Mareep
Flavor-Text: Its fleece grows continually. In the summer, the fleece is fully shed, but it grows back in a week.

Name: Flaaffy
Flavor-Text: Because of its rubbery, electricity-resistant skin, it can store lots of electricity in its fur.

Name: Ampharos
Effects: 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon is chosen at random. This attack also does 20 damage to it.
Flavor-Text: When it gets dark, the light from its bright, shiny tail can be seen from far away on the ocean’s surface.

Name: Mega Ampharos ex
Effects: 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon is chosen at random 3 times. For each time a Pokémon was chosen, also do 20 damage to it.

Name: Shinx
Flavor-Text: The extension and contraction of its muscles generates electricity. Its fur glows when it’s in trouble.

Name: Luxio
Flavor-Text: By gathering their tails together, they collectively generate powerful electricity from their claws.

Name: Luxray
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Luxray’s ability to see through objects comes in handy when it’s scouting for danger.

Name: Pachirisu
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has damage on it, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: A pair may be seen rubbing their cheek pouches together in an effort to share stored electricity.

Name: Blitzle
Flavor-Text: Its mane shines when it discharges electricity. They use the frequency and rhythm of these flashes to communicate.

Name: Zebstrika
Flavor-Text: They have lightning-like movements. When Zebstrika run at full speed, the sound of thunder reverberates.

Name: Joltik
Flavor-Text: Joltik can be found clinging to other Pokémon. It’s soaking up static electricity because it can’t produce a charge on its own.

Name: Galvantula
Flavor-Text: It lays traps of electrified threads near the nests of bird Pokémon, aiming to snare chicks that are not yet good at flying.

Name: Dedenne
Effects: Discard a [L] Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Dedenne emit electrical waves from the whiskers on their cheeks to communicate with each other. When low on electricity, they curl up and sleep.

Name: Yamper
Effects: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: This gluttonous Pokémon only assists people with their work because it wants treats. As it runs, it crackles with electricity.

Name: Boltund
Flavor-Text: It sends electricity through its legs to boost their strength. Running at top speed, it easily breaks 50 mph.

Name: Natu
Flavor-Text: Because its wings aren’t yet fully grown, it has to hop to get around. It is always staring at something.

Name: Xatu
Flavor-Text: This odd Pokémon can see both the past and the future. It eyes the sun’s movement all day.

Name: Misdreavus
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It loves to bite and yank people’s hair from behind without warning, just to see their shocked reactions.

Name: Mismagius
Flavor-Text: Its cries sound like incantations to torment the foe. It appears where you least expect it.

Name: Sableye
Effects: Discard a card from your hand. If you can't, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Materials from gems it has eaten float to the surface of its body and can form an infinite number of patterns among individuals.

Name: Mega Altaria ex
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.

Name: Duskull
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Making itself invisible, it silently sneaks up to prey. It has the ability to slip through thick walls.

Name: Dusclops
Flavor-Text: It seeks drifting will-o’-the-wisps and sucks them into its empty body. What happens inside is a mystery.

Name: Dusknoir
Ability-Effect: When this Pokémon is Knocked Out, flip a coin. If heads, your opponent can't get any points for it.
Flavor-Text: With the mouth on its belly, Dusknoir swallows its target whole. The soul is the only thing eaten— Dusknoir disgorges the body before departing.

Name: Jirachi
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is said to have the ability to grant any wish for just one week every thousand years.

Name: Drifloon
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Because of the way it floats aimlessly, an old folktale calls it a “signpost for wandering spirits.”

Name: Drifblim
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: They carry people and Pokémon, but the wind can catch them, so there can’t be a fixed destination.

Name: Chingling
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Item cards from their hand.
Flavor-Text: There is an orb inside its mouth. When it hops, the orb bounces all over and makes a ringing sound.

Name: Yamask
Flavor-Text: The spirit of a person from a bygone age became this Pokémon. It rambles through ruins, searching for someone who knows its face.

Name: Cofagrigus
Effects: Discard 2 cards from your hand. If you can't discard 2 cards, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: There are many depictions of Cofagrigus decorating ancient tombs. They’re symbols of the wealth that kings of bygone eras had.

Name: Gothita
Flavor-Text: Beware of touching the ribbon-shaped feelers that control its psychic power. Gothita will begin bawling if you do.

Name: Gothorita
Flavor-Text: Using its psychic power, it arranges pebbles to form the shapes of constellations. Some people believe this Pokémon came from outer space.

Name: Gothitelle
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, if they attach Energy from their Energy Zone to the Defending Pokémon, that Pokémon will be Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It gazes at the stars to predict the future. It acts somewhat detached because it has seen the end of all existence.

Name: Spritzee
Flavor-Text: The scent its body gives off enraptures those who smell it. Noble ladies had no shortage of love for Spritzee.

Name: Aromatisse
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: The scents Aromatisse can produce range from sweet smells that bolster allies to foul smells that sap an opponent’s will to fight.

Name: Swirlix
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text: The sweet smell of cotton candy perfumes Swirlix’s fluffy fur. This Pokémon spits out sticky string to tangle up its enemies.

Name: Slurpuff
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Slurpuff’s fur contains a lot of air, making it soft to the touch and lighter than it looks.

Name: Carbink
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −30 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text: It’s said that somewhere in the world, there’s a mineral vein housing a large pack of slumbering Carbink. It’s also said that this pack has a queen.

Name: Klefki
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon. If you do, discard this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: In the past, noble families entrusted their vault keys to a Klefki. They passed the Klefki down through the generations, taking good care of it.

Name: Indeedee ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may heal 20 damage from your Active Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

Name: Sandshrew
Flavor-Text: It burrows into the ground to create its nest. If hard stones impede its tunneling, it uses its sharp claws to shatter them and then carries on digging.

Name: Sandslash
Flavor-Text: It climbs trees by hooking on with its sharp claws. Sandslash shares the berries it gathers, dropping them down to Sandshrew waiting below the tree.

Name: Hitmonchan ex
Effects: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness.

Name: Sudowoodo
Effects: If this Pokémon has damage on it, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: To avoid being attacked, it does nothing but mimic a tree. It hates water and flees from rain.

Name: Makuhita
Flavor-Text: It toughens up by slamming into thick trees over and over. It gains a sturdy body and dauntless spirit.

Name: Hariyama
Effects: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It stomps on the ground to build power. It can send a 10-ton truck flying with a straight-arm punch.

Name: Hippopotas
Flavor-Text: It shrouds itself in sand to ward off germs. It travels easily through the sands of the desert.

Name: Hippowdon
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, during your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.
Flavor-Text: It brandishes its gaping mouth in a display of fearsome strength. It raises vast quantities of sand while attacking.

Name: Sandile
Flavor-Text: They live hidden under hot desert sands in order to keep their body temperature from dropping.

Name: Krokorok
Flavor-Text: Thanks to the special membrane covering its eyes, it can see its surroundings clearly, even in the middle of the night.

Name: Krookodile
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its unique faculty of sight can detect small prey more than 30 miles away, even in the midst of a sandstorm.

Name: Archen
Flavor-Text: Archen is said to be the ancestor of bird Pokémon. It lived in treetops, eating berries and bug Pokémon.

Name: Archeops
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. During your next turn, this Pokémon's Wild Spin attack does +20 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Though capable of flight, Archeops was apparently better at hunting on the ground.

Name: Golett
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon was created from clay. It received orders from its master many thousands of years ago, and it still follows those orders to this day.

Name: Golurk
Effects: Reveal the top 3 cards of your deck. This attack does 60 damage for each Pokémon with a Retreat Cost of 3 or more you find there. Shuffle the revealed cards back into your deck.
Flavor-Text: There’s a theory that inside Golurk is a perpetual motion machine that produces limitless energy, but this belief hasn’t been proven.

Name: Terrakion
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Sacred Sword.
Flavor-Text: It has phenomenal power. It will mercilessly crush anyone or anything that bullies small Pokémon.

Name: Pancham
Flavor-Text: Wanting to make sure it’s taken seriously, Pancham’s always giving others a glare. But if it’s not focusing, it ends up smiling.

Name: Crabrawler
Flavor-Text: If it loses its pincers, they’ll quickly regrow. The pincers are popular ingredients in paella since their shells produce a tasty soup stock.

Name: Crabominable
Effects: If this Pokémon's remaining HP is 30 or less, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Though its punches are powerful, this Pokémon’s movements are sluggish. It blows icy bubbles from its mouth to immobilize its opponents.

Name: Stufful
Flavor-Text: The way it protects itself by flailing its arms may be an adorable sight, but stay well away. This is flailing that can snap thick tree trunks.

Name: Bewear
Flavor-Text: The moves it uses to take down its prey would make a martial artist jealous. It tucks subdued prey under its arms to carry them to its nest.

Name: Sandygast
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to use an attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.
Flavor-Text: It flings sand into the eyes of its prey and tries to close in on them while they’re blinded, but since Sandygast is so slow, the prey tends to escape.

Name: Palossand
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: From the hollows in its arms, it fires the bones of its victims, which are all dried up after being drained of their vitality.

Name: Rolycoly
Flavor-Text: Chunks of the surface of this Pokémon’s body that have grown old and flaked off have long been used for fuel as an alternative to coal.

Name: Carkol
Flavor-Text: Due to the coal tar created inside it, the heap of coal on Carkol’s back never falls apart, even when the Pokémon rolls around at high speeds.

Name: Coalossal
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a [G] Pokémon, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It’s gentle usually but fearsome when angered. With a body that burns at over 2,700 degrees Fahrenheit, it crushes foes and turns them to ash.

Name: Murkrow
Flavor-Text: If spotted, it will lure an unwary person into chasing it, then lose the pursuer on mountain trails.

Name: Honchkrow
Effects: This attack does 40 more damage for each of your opponent's Pokémon in play that has an Ability.
Flavor-Text: A single cry from this nocturnal Pokémon, and more than 100 of its Murkrow cronies will assemble.

Name: Absol
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent reveals their hand. Choose a Supporter card you find there and discard it.
Flavor-Text: Because of this Pokémon’s ability to detect danger, people mistook Absol as a bringer of doom.

Name: Mega Absol ex
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand. Choose a Supporter card you find there and discard it.

Name: Skorupi
Flavor-Text: It attacks using the claws on its tail. Once locked in its grip, its prey is unable to move as this Pokémon’s poison seeps in.

Name: Drapion
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned and Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It’s so vicious that it’s called the Sand Demon. Yet when confronted by Hippowdon, Drapion keeps a low profile and will never pick a fight.

Name: Darkrai
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.
Flavor-Text: It can lull people to sleep and make them dream. It is active during nights of the new moon.

Name: Deino
Flavor-Text: It nests deep inside a cave. Food there is scarce, so Deino will sink its teeth into anything that moves and attempt to eat it.

Name: Zweilous
Flavor-Text: The two heads have different likes and dislikes. Because the heads fight with each other, Zweilous gets stronger without needing to rely on others.

Name: Hydreigon
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may take 2 [D] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon. If you do, do 30 damage to this Pokémon.
Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It’s said that Hydreigon grew ferocious because people in times long past loathed it, considering it to be evil incarnate and attacking it relentlessly.

Name: Pangoro
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Overdrive Smash attack does +30 damage.
Flavor-Text: Using its leaf, Pangoro can predict the moves of its opponents. It strikes with punches that can turn a dump truck into scrap with just one hit.

Name: Skrelp
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is a poor swimmer. If it’s caught in a fierce storm, it will sometimes get washed far away and become unable to return to its home.

Name: Dragalge ex
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, the Attacking Pokémon is now Poisoned.

Name: Mareanie
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It has excellent regenerative capabilities. Legs that a Mareanie has lost have a very bitter, astringent flavor—they’re not suitable as food.

Name: Toxapex
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Toxapex gets into fierce battles with Bruxish over areas where warm ocean currents flow, but the odds are always against it.

Name: Impidimp
Flavor-Text: They live in groups, pestering and playing pranks on each other to polish their troublemaking skills.

Name: Morgrem
Effects: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon absorbs negative emotions and turns them into energy. It’s popular with people who tend to think gloomy thoughts.

Name: Grimmsnarl
Flavor-Text: It lives deep within the forest. Even after evolving into this splendid form, it hasn’t given up on its petty misdeeds and pranks.

Name: Ferroseed
Flavor-Text: Mossy caves are their preferred dwellings. Enzymes contained in mosses help Ferroseed’s spikes grow big and strong.

Name: Ferrothorn
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text: Its spikes are harder than steel. This Pokémon crawls across rock walls by stabbing the spikes on its feelers into the stone.

Name: Durant
Effects: If Durant is on your Bench, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: They lay their eggs deep inside their nests. When attacked by Heatmor, they retaliate using their massive mandibles.

Name: Cobalion
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Sacred Sword.
Flavor-Text: It has a body and heart of steel. It worked with its allies to punish people when they hurt Pokémon.

Name: Honedge
Flavor-Text: The blue eye on the sword’s handguard is the true body of Honedge. With its old cloth, it drains people’s lives away.

Name: Doublade
Flavor-Text: The two swords employ a strategy of rapidly alternating between offense and defense to bring down their prey.

Name: Aegislash
Ability-Effect: Attacks used by your [P] Pokémon and [M] Pokémon do +30 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Once upon a time, a king with an Aegislash reigned over the land. His Pokémon eventually drained him of life, and his kingdom fell with him.

Name: Meltan
Flavor-Text: It dissolves and eats metal. Circulating liquid metal within its body is how it generates energy.

Name: Melmetal ex
Effects: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 50 more damage.

Name: Corviknight
Effects: Discard 2 [M] Energy from this Pokémon. During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −50 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text: Although its wings have partly turned to steel and become heavy as a result, this Pokémon flies through the skies with ease.

Name: Druddigon
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Druddigon are vicious and cunning. They take up residence in nests dug out by other Pokémon, treating the stolen nests as their own lairs.

Name: Goomy
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, attacks used by your opponent's Active Pokémon cost 1 [C] more.
Flavor-Text: Goomy’s horns are excellent sensory organs that cover all five of Goomy’s senses. Goomy picks up on danger from the movement of the air.

Name: Sliggoo
Flavor-Text: When Sliggoo senses danger, the mucus coating its entire body becomes more concentrated— it’ll dissolve anything.

Name: Goodra
Effects: Heal 40 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is uncontrollable when enraged. It rampages on and on, lashing its tail with enough power to send a dump truck flying.

Name: Pidgey
Flavor-Text: It is docile and prefers to avoid conflict. If disturbed, however, it can ferociously strike back.

Name: Pidgeotto
Flavor-Text: Very protective of its sprawling territorial area, this Pokémon will fiercely peck at any intruder.

Name: Pidgeot
Effects: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon. If both of them are tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It spreads its gorgeous wings widely to intimidate enemies. It races through the skies at Mach-2 speed.

Name: Tauros ex
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

Name: Eevee
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, it can evolve during your first turn or the turn you play it.
Flavor-Text: Its genetic code is irregular. It may mutate if it is exposed to radiation from element stones.

Name: Aipom
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It searches for prey from the tops of trees. When it spots its favorite food, Bounsweet, Aipom gets excited and pounces.

Name: Ambipom
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads. If this Pokémon has Lucky Mittens attached, flip 4 coins instead.
Flavor-Text: It uses its tails for everything. If it wraps both of its tails around you and gives you a squeeze, that’s proof it really likes you.

Name: Miltank
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon needs to be milked every day, or else it will fall ill. The flavor of Miltank milk changes with the seasons.

Name: Zigzagoon
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon with abundant curiosity. It shows an interest in everything, so it always zigzags.

Name: Linoone
Flavor-Text: It uses its explosive speed and razor-sharp claws to bring down prey. Running along winding paths is not its strong suit.

Name: Whismur
Flavor-Text: When Whismur cries, the sound of its own voice startles it, making the Pokémon cry even louder. It cries until it’s exhausted, then it falls asleep.

Name: Loudred
Flavor-Text: The force of this Pokémon’s loud voice isn’t just the sound—it’s also the wave of air pressure that blows opponents away and damages them.

Name: Exploud
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Item cards from their hand.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon can do more than just shout. To communicate with others of its kind, it’ll emit all sorts of sounds from the holes in its body.

Name: Skitty
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It shows its cute side by chasing its own tail until it gets dizzy.

Name: Delcatty
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may put a Supporter card from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: It is highly popular among female Trainers for its sublime fur. It does not keep a nest.

Name: Spinda
Effects: Both Active Pokémon are now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Each Spinda’s spot pattern is different. With its stumbling movements, it evades opponents’ attacks brilliantly!

Name: Swablu
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It can’t relax if it or its surroundings are not clean. It wipes off dirt with its wings.

Name: Altaria
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: On sunny days, it flies freely through the sky and blends into the clouds. It sings in a beautiful soprano.

Name: Chatot
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It can learn and speak human words. If they gather, they all learn the same saying.

Name: Patrat
Flavor-Text: Extremely cautious, one of them will always be on the lookout, but it won’t notice a foe coming from behind.

Name: Watchog
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: When they see an enemy, their tails stand high, and they spit the seeds of berries stored in their cheek pouches.

Name: Lillipup
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is far brighter than the average child, and Lillipup won’t forget the love it receives or any abuse it suffers.

Name: Herdier
Flavor-Text: The black fur that covers this Pokémon’s body is dense and springy. Even sharp fangs bounce right off.

Name: Stoutland
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Stoutland is immensely proud of its impressive moustache. It’s said that moustache length is what determines social standing among this species.

Name: Rufflet
Flavor-Text: With its sharp claws, this Pokémon pierces its prey, and then it pecks at them. Although it also consumes berries, it’s a carnivore at heart.

Name: Braviary
Flavor-Text: For the sake of its friends, this brave warrior of the sky will not stop battling, even if injured.

Name: Furfrou
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text: Left alone, its fur will grow longer and longer, but it will only allow someone it trusts to cut it.

Name: Furfrou
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text: Left alone, its fur will grow longer and longer, but it will only allow someone it trusts to cut it.

Name: Furfrou
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text: Left alone, its fur will grow longer and longer, but it will only allow someone it trusts to cut it.

Name: Rookidee
Flavor-Text: The females are fussier than the males. If another creature dirties a female Rookidee’s wings, it’ll peck the offender relentlessly in a burning rage.

Name: Corvisquire
Flavor-Text: This intelligent Pokémon will quickly learn how to use any tool it can hold in its beak or its talons.

Name: Wooloo
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: If its fleece grows too long, Wooloo won’t be able to move. Cloth made with the wool of this Pokémon is surprisingly strong.

Name: Dubwool
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.
Flavor-Text: Its majestic horns are meant only to impress the opposite gender. They never see use in battle.

Name: Prank Spinner
Description: A card from among both player's hands is chosen at random, revealed to the other player, and shuffled into its owner's deck.

Name: Plume Fossil
Description: Play this card as if it were a 40-HP Basic [C] Pokémon.At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.This card can't retreat.

Name: Hitting Hammer
Description: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

Name: Cover Fossil
Description: Play this card as if it were a 40-HP Basic [C] Pokémon.At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.This card can't retreat.

Name: Flame Patch
Description: Attach a [R] Energy from your discard pile to your Active [R] Pokémon.

Name: Sitrus Berry
Description: At the end of each turn, if the Pokémon this card is attached to has half of its maximum HP or less remaining, heal 30 damage from it. If you do, discard this card.

Name: Heavy Helmet
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to has a Retreat Cost of 3 or more, it takes −20 damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon.

Name: Lucky Mittens
Description: Whenever your opponent's Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack used by the Pokémon this card is attached to, draw a card.

Name: Marlon
Description: Heal 70 damage from 1 of your Carracosta or Jellicent.

Name: Hala
Description: During your opponent's next turn, if your Hariyama or Crabominable would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, it is not Knocked Out and its remaining HP becomes 10.

Name: May
Description: Put 2 random Pokémon from your deck into your hand. For each Pokémon you put into your hand in this way, choose a Pokémon to shuffle from your hand into your deck.

Name: Fantina
Description: Take a [P] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to each of your Drifblim and Mismagius.

Name: Copycat
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Draw a card for each card in your opponent's hand.

Name: Lisia
Description: Put 2 random Basic Pokémon with 50 HP or less from your deck into your hand.

Name: Beautifly
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has an aggressive nature. It stabs prey with its long, narrow mouth to drain the prey’s fluids.

Name: Skiddo
Flavor-Text: As long as it has sunlight and water, Skiddo can make energy with the leaves on its body, allowing it to live on rocky mountains barren of food.

Name: Rillaboom
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may flip a coin. If heads, switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.
Flavor-Text: The one with the best drumming techniques becomes the boss of the troop. It has a gentle disposition and values harmony among its group.

Name: Growlithe
Flavor-Text: It’s very friendly and faithful to people. It will try to repel enemies by barking and biting.

Name: Chandelure
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may discard the top card of your opponent's deck.
Flavor-Text: In homes illuminated by Chandelure instead of lights, funerals were a constant occurrence— or so it’s said.

Name: Magikarp
Effects: Put a random card from your deck that evolves from this Pokémon onto this Pokémon to evolve it.
Flavor-Text: In the distant past, it was somewhat stronger than the horribly weak descendants that exist today.

Name: Ludicolo
Effects: If you have exactly 1, 3, or 5 cards in your hand, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: If it hears festive music, it begins moving in rhythm in order to amplify its power.

Name: Jellicent
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, take a [W] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Whenever a full moon hangs in the night sky, schools of Jellicent gather near the surface of the sea, waiting for their prey to appear.

Name: Keldeo
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Sacred Sword.
Flavor-Text: It crosses the world, running over the surfaces of oceans and rivers. It appears at scenic waterfronts.

Name: Eiscue
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has full HP, it takes −40 damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: If you lick the ice covering its face, you’ll find it has a faintly salty taste. This species rode here on ocean currents from a cold, faraway land.

Name: Luxray
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Luxray’s ability to see through objects comes in handy when it’s scouting for danger.

Name: Cofagrigus
Effects: Discard 2 cards from your hand. If you can't discard 2 cards, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: There are many depictions of Cofagrigus decorating ancient tombs. They’re symbols of the wealth that kings of bygone eras had.

Name: Gothita
Flavor-Text: Beware of touching the ribbon-shaped feelers that control its psychic power. Gothita will begin bawling if you do.

Name: Makuhita
Flavor-Text: It toughens up by slamming into thick trees over and over. It gains a sturdy body and dauntless spirit.

Name: Hippowdon
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, during your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.
Flavor-Text: It brandishes its gaping mouth in a display of fearsome strength. It raises vast quantities of sand while attacking.

Name: Archeops
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. During your next turn, this Pokémon's Wild Spin attack does +20 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Though capable of flight, Archeops was apparently better at hunting on the ground.

Name: Pancham
Flavor-Text: Wanting to make sure it’s taken seriously, Pancham’s always giving others a glare. But if it’s not focusing, it ends up smiling.

Name: Honchkrow
Effects: This attack does 40 more damage for each of your opponent's Pokémon in play that has an Ability.
Flavor-Text: A single cry from this nocturnal Pokémon, and more than 100 of its Murkrow cronies will assemble.

Name: Hydreigon
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may take 2 [D] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon. If you do, do 30 damage to this Pokémon.
Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It’s said that Hydreigon grew ferocious because people in times long past loathed it, considering it to be evil incarnate and attacking it relentlessly.

Name: Corviknight
Effects: Discard 2 [M] Energy from this Pokémon. During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −50 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text: Although its wings have partly turned to steel and become heavy as a result, this Pokémon flies through the skies with ease.

Name: Goomy
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, attacks used by your opponent's Active Pokémon cost 1 [C] more.
Flavor-Text: Goomy’s horns are excellent sensory organs that cover all five of Goomy’s senses. Goomy picks up on danger from the movement of the air.

Name: Delcatty
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may put a Supporter card from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: It is highly popular among female Trainers for its sublime fur. It does not keep a nest.

Name: Stoutland
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Stoutland is immensely proud of its impressive moustache. It’s said that moustache length is what determines social standing among this species.

Name: Rufflet
Flavor-Text: With its sharp claws, this Pokémon pierces its prey, and then it pecks at them. Although it also consumes berries, it’s a carnivore at heart.

Name: Mega Pinsir ex
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 70 more damage.

Name: Whimsicott ex
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

Name: Rapidash ex
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

Name: Mega Blaziken ex
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy from this Pokémon. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

Name: Mega Gyarados ex
Effects: Discard the top 3 cards of your opponent's deck.

Name: Greninja ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may switch your Active [W] Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

Name: Jolteon ex
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, whenever your opponent attaches an Energy from their Energy Zone to 1 of their Pokémon, do 20 damage to that Pokémon.

Name: Mega Ampharos ex
Effects: 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon is chosen at random 3 times. For each time a Pokémon was chosen, also do 20 damage to it.

Name: Mega Altaria ex
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.

Name: Indeedee ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may heal 20 damage from your Active Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

Name: Hitmonchan ex
Effects: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness.

Name: Mega Absol ex
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand. Choose a Supporter card you find there and discard it.

Name: Dragalge ex
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, the Attacking Pokémon is now Poisoned.

Name: Melmetal ex
Effects: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 50 more damage.

Name: Tauros ex
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

Description: During your opponent's next turn, if your Hariyama or Crabominable would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, it is not Knocked Out and its remaining HP becomes 10.

Description: Put 2 random Pokémon from your deck into your hand. For each Pokémon you put into your hand in this way, choose a Pokémon to shuffle from your hand into your deck.

Name: Fantina
Description: Take a [P] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to each of your Drifblim and Mismagius.

Name: Copycat
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Draw a card for each card in your opponent's hand.

Name: Lisia
Description: Put 2 random Basic Pokémon with 50 HP or less from your deck into your hand.

Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 70 more damage.

Name: Whimsicott ex
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

Name: Rapidash ex
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

Name: Greninja ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may switch your Active [W] Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

Name: Jolteon ex
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, whenever your opponent attaches an Energy from their Energy Zone to 1 of their Pokémon, do 20 damage to that Pokémon.

Name: Mega Ampharos ex
Effects: 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon is chosen at random 3 times. For each time a Pokémon was chosen, also do 20 damage to it.

Name: Indeedee ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may heal 20 damage from your Active Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

Name: Hitmonchan ex
Effects: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness.

Name: Mega Absol ex
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand. Choose a Supporter card you find there and discard it.

Name: Dragalge ex
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, the Attacking Pokémon is now Poisoned.

Name: Melmetal ex
Effects: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 50 more damage.

Name: Tauros ex
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

Name: Mega Blaziken ex
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy from this Pokémon. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

Name: Mega Gyarados ex
Effects: Discard the top 3 cards of your opponent's deck.

Name: Mega Altaria ex
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.

Name: Bellsprout
Flavor-Text: Even though its body is extremely skinny, it is blindingly fast when catching its prey.

Name: Weepinbell
Flavor-Text: The leafy parts act as cutters for slashing foes. It spits a fluid that dissolves everything.

Name: Victreebel
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, once during your turn, you may switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Basic Pokémon to the Active Spot.
Flavor-Text: Said to live in huge colonies deep in jungles, although no one has ever returned from there.

Name: Rowlet
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It sends its feathers, which are as sharp as blades, flying in attack. Its legs are strong, so its kicks are also formidable.

Name: Dartrix
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This narcissistic Pokémon is a clean freak. If you don’t groom it diligently, it may stop listening to you.

Name: Moltres
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It’s one of the legendary bird Pokémon. When Moltres flaps its flaming wings, they glimmer with a dazzling red glow.

Name: Litten
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: If you try too hard to get close to it, it won’t open up to you. Even if you do grow close, giving it too much affection is still a no-no.

Name: Torracat
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Torracat will let its Trainer coddle it once they’ve grown close, but it’s a powerful, sharp-clawed Pokémon, so its Trainer gets covered in scratches.

Name: Poliwag
Effects: Put 1 random Poliwag from your deck onto your Bench.
Flavor-Text: For Poliwag, swimming is easier than walking. The swirl pattern on its belly is actually part of the Pokémon’s innards showing through the skin.

Name: Poliwhirl
Flavor-Text: Staring at the swirl on its belly causes drowsiness. This trait of Poliwhirl’s has been used in place of lullabies to get children to go to sleep.

Name: Poliwrath
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, do 20 damage to the Attacking Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its body is solid muscle. When swimming through cold seas, Poliwrath uses its impressive arms to smash through drift ice and plow forward.

Name: Articuno
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It’s said that this Pokémon’s beautiful blue wings are made of ice. Articuno flies over snowy mountains, its long tail fluttering along behind it.

Name: Manaphy
Effects: Choose 2 of your Benched Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, take a [W] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is born with a wondrous power that lets it bond with any kind of Pokémon.

Name: Popplio
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: The balloons it inflates with its nose grow larger and larger as it practices day by day.

Name: Brionne
Flavor-Text: It gets excited when it sees a dance it doesn’t know. This hard worker practices diligently until it can learn that dance.

Name: Zapdos
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon has complete control over electricity. There are tales of Zapdos nesting in the dark depths of pitch-black thunderclouds.

Name: Oricorio
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon ex.
Flavor-Text: This form of Oricorio has sipped yellow nectar. It uses nimble steps to approach opponents, then knocks them out with electric punches.

Name: Zeraora
Ability-Effect: At the end of your first turn, take a [L] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It electrifies its claws and tears its opponents apart with them. Even if they dodge its attack, they’ll be electrocuted by the flying sparks.

Name: Drowzee
Flavor-Text: It remembers every dream it eats. It rarely eats the dreams of adults because children’s are much tastier.

Name: Hypno
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: When it locks eyes with an enemy, it will use a mix of psi moves, such as Hypnosis and Confusion.

Name: Geodude
Flavor-Text: Geodude that have lived a long life have had all their edges smoothed out until they’re totally round. They also have a calm, quiet disposition.

Name: Graveler
Flavor-Text: It climbs up cliffs as it heads toward the peak of a mountain. As soon as it reaches the summit, it rolls back down the way it came.

Name: Golem
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −30 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text: When Golem grow old, they stop shedding their shells. Those that have lived a long, long time have shells green with moss.

Name: Rockruff
Effects: Put a random card that evolves from Rockruff from your deck into your hand.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is very friendly when it’s young. Its disposition becomes vicious once it matures, but it never forgets the kindness of its master.

Name: Alolan Diglett
Flavor-Text: The metal-rich geology of this Pokémon’s habitat caused it to develop steel whiskers on its head.

Name: Meowth
Effects: Draw 1 card.
Flavor-Text: All it does is sleep during the daytime. At night, it patrols its territory with its eyes aglow.

Name: Persian
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a random card from your opponent's hand.
Flavor-Text: Although its fur has many admirers, it is tough to raise as a pet because of its fickle meanness.

Name: Doduo
Flavor-Text: A two-headed Pokémon that was discovered as a  sudden mutation. It runs at a pace of over 60 miles per hour.

Name: Dodrio
Flavor-Text: An enemy that takes its eyes off any of the three heads—even for a second—will get pecked severely.

Name: Bidoof
Effects: Halve your opponent's Active Pokémon's remaining HP, rounded down.
Flavor-Text: With nerves of steel, nothing can perturb it. It is more agile and active than it appears.

Name: Decidueye ex
Effects: This attack does 100 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon that have damage on them.

Name: Incineroar ex
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.
If this Pokémon has damage on it, this attack does 60 more damage.

Name: Palkia ex
Effects: Discard 3 [W] Energy from this Pokémon. This attack also does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

Name: Primarina ex
Effects: If this Pokémon has at least 1 extra [W] Energy attached, this attack does 40 more damage.
Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

Name: Pikachu ex
Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

Name: Tapu Koko ex
Effects: Take a [L] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.

Name: Lycanroc ex
Effects: Discard a [F] Energy from this Pokémon.

Name: Passimian ex
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, move all [F] Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

Name: Alolan Dugtrio ex
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.

Name: Dialga ex
Effects: Take 2 [M] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

Name: Bibarel ex
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

Name: Arceus ex
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can't be affected by any Special Conditions.
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.

Name: Lilligant
Ability-Effect: Each of your [G] Pokémon gets +20 HP.
Flavor-Text: No matter how much time and money is spent raising it, its flowers are the most beautiful when they bloom in the wild.

Name: Klefki
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon. If you do, discard this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: In the past, noble families entrusted their vault keys to a Klefki. They passed the Klefki down through the generations, taking good care of it.

Name: Flame Patch
Description: Attach a [R] Energy from your discard pile to your Active [R] Pokémon.
